personality_one_liner: A confident, offline-first productivity toolkit that feels as refined and purposeful as native iOS apps—no clutter, just absolute clarity.

spacing.rule: Generous vertical breathing room. Use 24pt (xl) outer screen padding. Components should feel distinctly grouped with clear whitespace, never cramped.

do's: Use glass on the bottom tab bar
Use glass on sticky headers over scrolling content
Use glass overlay on the Hero/Tips card on the Home and Government screens
Use a glass/frosted container for the floating primary CTA or FAB
dont's: Do not use glass on regular list rows
Do not use glass on dense data forms
Do not apply blur without a tinted background layer to ensure readability

screens.name: Home
screens.purpose: The command center for document prep—fast access to scanning, quick tools, and recent files.

states.loading: Centered spinner with a subtle 'Preparing workspace...' text.
states.empty: Empty state for Recent Files: minimal illustration (minimalist desk photo placeholder) with 'No recent documents' and a CTA to 'Scan Document'.
states.error: Card showing 'Unable to load recent files' with a Retry button.

screens.layout:
1. Safe area top header with 'DocMate' wordmark (Geist 500) and Settings icon.
2. Search Bar: full-width minus padding, surfaceSecondary bg.
3. Tips/Hero Card: Top section featuring an abstract minimal paper background (use provided URL), dark gradient scrim, and 'Prepare your Aadhaar & PAN perfectly' text with a primary CTA.
4. Quick Actions: Horizontal scroll (flex-direction: row, gap: md) of square cards (Scanner, Compress, Sign) with brandTertiary tinted icon containers.
5. Government Services Grid: 2-column flexWrap grid (space-between) for fast preset access.
6. Recent Files: Vertical list of standard list items.
7. Ad Banner Placeholder: Fixed container at bottom, surfaceSecondary bg, 1pt border, text 'Ad Placement'.

screens.name: Tools
screens.purpose: Comprehensive index of all available toolkit utilities grouped by category.

states.loading: Skeleton rows mirroring the list layout.

screens.layout: Vertical scrollview. Sections: 'Image Tools', 'PDF Tools', 'Signature'. Section headers use Geist 500 at xl size. List rows: leading icon inside a brandTertiary rounded square, title, subtitle, trailing chevron. Ad banner placeholder at the very bottom.

screens.name: Government
screens.purpose: Specific workflows tailored to exact government document constraints.
screens.layout: Hero banner at top using minimalist desk photo (with gradient scrim). Below it, a vertical list of large cards (Passport, PAN, DL, Scholarships). Each card shows the exact constraints (e.g., 'Max 200KB • JPEG'). Tapping a card opens a Bottom Sheet containing the specific requirements and a full-width sticky 'Prepare Document' CTA.

screens.name: History
screens.purpose: Central repository for all processed documents, entirely offline.

states.empty: Full-screen flex: 1 empty state. Minimalist paper illustration, 'Your history is empty', and a 'Scan First Document' primary CTA.
states.error: Text 'Could not load storage' + Retry CTA.

screens.layout: Sticky glass header with Search and Filter/Sort icons. Vertical FlatList of document cards. Each card: thumbnail on left (surfaceTertiary fallback), Title, Date, Size, Type badge (brandTertiary), and a 3-dot vertical menu icon. Bottom ad banner placeholder.

screens.name: Settings
screens.purpose: App preferences, offline storage management, and information.
screens.layout: Standard iOS-style grouped list rows. Sections: 'Preferences' (Dark mode toggle, Language), 'Storage' (Local space used + 'Clear All' button), 'About'. Ad banner placeholder at bottom.

screens.name: SmartScannerOverlay
screens.purpose: Camera overlay for document edge detection and capture.

states.error: Camera permission denied message + 'Open Settings' CTA.

screens.layout: Full-screen camera view. Top safe area: back button, flash toggle, auto-scan toggle. Overlay: green (success) or brand-colored borders tracking document edges. Bottom safe area: solid black or highly tinted glass background, large primary capture button (pill shape, brandPrimary), gallery picker icon on left.

screens.name: ToolDetail_Compressor
screens.purpose: Focus screen for adjusting image size precisely.

states.error: Toast: 'Image too small to compress further'.

screens.layout: Header: Title 'Compress Image'. Content: Image preview container (centered, surfaceSecondary bg). Below: segmented control or wrapping chip group for presets (10KB, 50KB, 100KB, 500KB, Custom). Text showing 'Estimated Size: ~45KB'. Sticky primary CTA at bottom 'Compress & Save'.

hero_abstract_paper.alt: Clean abstract minimal paper rolls in soft cream tones

hero_minimal_desk.alt: Top view of a minimalist workspace with laptop and paper on a white table

empty_state_desk.alt: Minimalist desk layout with paper and stationery

instructions_to_main_agent: IMPLEMENTATION RULE: Ensure completely offline functionality. Do not implement any API calls or backend syncs. Use AsyncStorage or SQLite for history.
STYLING: Strictly follow the 1 iOS-Native Clean personality. Do not use any blue, indigo, or purple hues. Stick to the Sage Green (brand) and grayscale palette.
LAYOUT: Use the defined Safe Areas meticulously. Ensure all screens have a 24pt (xl) outer padding unless they are full-bleed images.
CTA PATTERN: Every tool workflow (Compressor, Scanner, Signature) must feature a sticky primary CTA at the bottom of the screen, anchored above the home indicator, unaffected by scroll.

IMAGE HANDLING: Any time an image from the 'images' block is used as a background or hero banner, you MUST overlay a vertical linear-gradient scrim (transparent at top, 70%+ surfaceInverse at bottom) and place the text in the bottom 30% to guarantee WCAG contrast.

AD BANNERS: Create a distinct component called `AdBannerPlaceholder` that matches the exact visual description (surfaceSecondary, 1pt border, text centered) and place it at the bottom of Home, History, and Settings as requested.

NAVIGATION: Implement a 5-tab bottom navigation using expo-router or react-navigation. Ensure inactive tabs are outlined Lucide icons, and the active tab is a filled/highlighted Lucide icon (or use stroke width emphasis if filled is unavailable).
